1.subject: Short title, extent and commencement.
1.comparison_summary: It contains the Act name, Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023, and provisions regarding the commencement date.

2.subject: Definitions.
2.comparison_summary: Several new subsections, such as 2(1)(a), 2(1)(b), 2(1)(d), and 2(1)(e), are added, while others, namely 2(f), 2(k), 2(q), and 2(t) of CrPC, are excluded. Furthermore, certain subsections are modified.

2(1)(l).subject: "investigation".
2(1)(l).comparison_summary: The term "Code" has been substituted with "Sanhita". Explanation that in case of inconsistency provisions of Special Act shall prevail.

2(1)(r).subject: "officer in charge of a police station".

2(1)(y).subject: "victim".
2(1)(y).comparison_summary: The words "for which accused person has been charged and the expression victim” are replaced by "of the accused person" etc.

2(2).subject: Words and expressions used.
2(2).comparison_summary: Words and expressions used in Sanhita but not defined here but defined in Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023, and in the Information Technology Act, 2000, shall have the meanings respectively assigned to them in that Act and Sanhita.

4.subject: Trial of offences under Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and other laws.

6.subject: Classes of Criminal Courts.
6.comparison_summary: Words "in any Metropolitan area, Metropolitan Magistrates" are excluded.

7.subject: Territorial divisions.
7.comparison_summary: Proviso related to Metropolitan area, excluded.

9.subject: Courts of Judicial Magistrates.
9.comparison_summary: The words "not being a Metropolitan area" are excluded.

10.subject: Chief Judicial Magistrate and Additional Chief Judicial Magistrate, etc.

12.subject: Local jurisdiction of Judicial Magistrates.
12.comparison_summary: The words "Metropolitan area" and Chief Metropolitan Magistrate are excluded.

14.subject: Executive Magistrates.
14.comparison_summary: The words "in any Metropolitan area, Metropolitan Magistrates" are excluded.

15.subject: Special Executive Magistrates.
15.comparison_summary: With the new addition, State Government may appoint police officers of the rank Superintendent of Police or e quivalent, as Special Executive Magistrates.

17.subject: Subordination of Executive Magistrates.
17.comparison_summary: The words "other than the additional District Magistrate" are excluded.

18.subject: Public Prosecutors.
18.comparison_summary: New proviso is added to subsection (1).

19.comparison_summary: The time line of fourteen days for notice to the state government for the appointment of Assistant Public Prosecutor is mentioned. Proviso is reframed but the meaning is same.

20.subject: Directorate of Prosecution.
20.comparison_summary: The new addition subsection (1) (b) regarding establishment of District Directorate of Prosecution. Subsections (8), (9), (10) and (11) are also newly added etc.

21.subject: Courts by which offences are triable.

22.subject: Sentences which High Courts and Sessions Judges may pass.
22.comparison_summary: Sub Section 28(3) of CrPC is excluded.

23.subject: Sentences which Magistrates may pass.
23.comparison_summary: Change in amount of fine: ten thousand is replaced by fifty thousand, and five thousand is replaced by ten thousand. The explanation of Section 23 defines "community service." S ub-section 29(4) CrPC is excluded.

24.subject: Sentence of imprisonment in default of fine.

25.subject: Sentence in cases of conviction of several offences at one trial.
25.comparison_summary: Sub section (1) is reframed but the essence is same. In subsection (2) (a) fourteen years is replaced by twenty years.

29.subject: Powers of Judges and Magistrates exercisable by their successors-in-office.
29.comparison_summary: The words "Additional or Assistant session Judge" are excluded.

34.comparison_summary: In subsection (2) (ii), instead of writing BNS sections corresponding to IPC sections, the offence punishable for imprisonment of ten years or more, etc., is mentioned. .

35.subject: When police may arrest without warrant.
35.comparison_summary: A new subsection 7 is added: “No arrest shall be made without prior permission of an officer not below the rank of Deputy Superintendent of Police in case of an offence which is punishable for imprisonment of less than three years and such person is infirm or is above sixty years of age.”

36.subject: Procedure of arrest and duties of officer making arrest.
36.comparison_summary: The words "any other person" is added are clause (c).

37.subject: Designated Police Officer.
37.comparison_summary: Sub-sections (2) and (3) of the CrPC are merged in (1) (b), and provision is given for designating a police officer not below the rank of Assistant Sub-Inspector for the said p urpose.

38.subject: Right of arrested person to meet an advocate of his choice during interrogation.

39.subject: Arrest on refusal to give name and residence.
39.comparison_summary: The words "his executing a bond with or without sureties" are replaced by "bond or bail bond," .

40.subject: Arrest by private person and procedure on such arrest.
40.comparison_summary: In subsection (1), without unnecessary delay is further specified by "but within six hours from such arrest." In subsection (2), “re-arrest” is replaced by "take him in custody".

42.subject: Protection of members of the Armed Forces from arrest.

43.subject: Arrest how made.
43.comparison_summary: A new subsection (3) is added regarding the handcuff of a habitual or repeat offender or accused who has committed certain offences mentioned in the sub-section, etc.

44.subject: Search of place entered by person sought to be arrested.

49.subject: Search of arrested person.
49.comparison_summary: No Change except the word "whenever" is excluded from clause (ii) of subsection (1).

50.comparison_summary: The words "immediately after the arrest is made" are added.

51.subject: Examination of accused by medical practitioner at the request of police officer.
51.comparison_summary: From sub-section (1), "not below the rank of sub-inspector" is excluded, and subsection (3) is newly added regarding the forwarding of examination reports without delay by a medical practitioner to the investigator. In explanation (b), the "defined in clause (h) of Section 2 of the Indian Medical Council Act, 1956 (102 of 1956)" is replaced by "recognised under the National Medical Commission Act, 2019 (30 of 2019)," and "the National Medical Register" is also added as an optional to the State Medical Register.

52.comparison_summary: The words "police officer not below the rank of Sub- Inspector" are replaced by "any police officer".

53.comparison_summary: A new proviso added to subsection (1) regarding one more examination can be done if it is necessary, in the opinion of the medical practitioner.

54.subject: Identification of person arrested.
54.comparison_summary: The second proviso is excluded as it is already covered under the first proviso by adding the words "the identification process shall be recorded by any audio-video electronic means.".

55.subject: Procedure when police officer deputes subordinate to arrest without warrant.

56.subject: Health and safety of arrested person.

58.comparison_summary: No change except the words "whether having jurisdiction or not" are added.

62.subject: Arrest to be made strictly according to the Sanhita.

63.comparison_summary: A new clause (ii) “in an encrypted or any other form of electronic communication and shall bear the image of the seal of the Court or digital signature” is added.

64.subject: Summons how served.
64.comparison_summary: Two new provisos regarding electronic communication, under subsections (1) and (2), are added. The word "personally" is added in sub-section (3).

65.subject: Service of summons on corporate bodies, firms, and societies.
65.comparison_summary: In subsection (1), Director and Manager is added. New subsection (2) regarding service of summons on any partner of a firm or other association of individuals.

67.subject: Procedure when service cannot be effected as before provided.

69.subject: Service of summons outside local limits.

70.comparison_summary: A new subsection (3) is added, mentioning that service of summons through electronic communication shall be considered duly served, and a copy of such summons shall be attested and kept as proof of service of summons.

71.subject: Service of summons on witness by post.
71.comparison_summary: The words "by electronic communication or" are added in subsection (1). In subsection (2) words "or on the proof of delivery of summons under sub-section (3) of section 70 by electronic communication to the satisfaction of the Court," are added.

73.subject: Power to direct security to be taken.

83.comparison_summary: The word "bail" is replaced by "bail bond".

84.comparison_summary: In subsection (4), IPC sections are replaced by punishments related to corresponding BNS sections. the offence punishable for imprisonment of ten years or more, etc., is mentioned.

85.comparison_summary: The words "of property " is added in clause (b) of subsection (1).

94.comparison_summary: Regarding the production of documents, the words "electronic communication, including communication devices which is likely to contain digital evidence " are added, and the words "either in physical form or in electronic form, require" are related to summoning a person possessing a document. In subsection (3) (a) corresponding sections of Indian Evidence Act, 1872 are replaced by the sections of the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam, 2023 , word "telegram is excluded.

97.comparison_summary: In clause (a) of subsection (2), "the Metal Token Act, 1889 (1 of 1889)" is replaced by "the Coinage Act, 2011 (11 of 2011)." BNS sections replace the corresponding "IPC" s ections.

In Subsection 2, "Magistrate" is replaced by "Executive Magistrate".

173.subject: Information in cognizable cases.
173.comparison_summary: By adding the words "irrespective of the area where the offence is committed”, the scope is expanded to lodge an FIR. This corresponds to the concept of ‘zero FIR’By adding the word "by electronic communication," thereby it is made contemporary. Clause (ii) is added in subsection (1); this is known as eFIR.Subsection (3) is newly added regarding preliminary inquiry to ascertain whether there exists a prima facie case and proceed for investigation on the prima facie case for the cognizable offences which are made punishable for three years or more but less than seven years".In subsection (4), the words "failing which such aggrieved person may make an application to the Magistrate." are added.

174.subject: Information as to non- cognizable cases and investigation of such cases.
174.comparison_summary: Clause (ii) in subsection (1) is added regarding forwarding the daily diary report of non-cognizable cases fortnightly to the Magistrate.

175.subject: Police officer's power to investigate cognizable case.
175.comparison_summary: A new proviso to subsection (1) is added. Sub-section (4) is newly added to protect public servants in the course of discharge of public duty against undue harassment, and subsection (3) is also amended according to sub-section(4) of section 173.

176.subject: Procedure for investigation.
176.comparison_summary: In clause (b) of subsection (1), audio-video electronic means are added. In subsection (2), the words, "forward the daily diary report fortnightly to the Magistrate" are added. Sub-section (3) is newly added, which stipulates forensic experts visit the crime scene as mandatory for offences punishable for seven years or more.

179.subject: Police officer's power to require attendance of witnesses.
179.comparison_summary: In the first proviso, "sixty-five years" is replaced by "sixty years" and the words "or a person with acute illness" are added. One new proviso is added. Thus, the scope of the provision is expanded.

181.subject: Statements to police and use thereof.
181.comparison_summary: Heading of the section is changed. BSA sections replace corresponding Indian Evidence Act sections.

183.subject: Recording of confessions and statements.
183.comparison_summary: The newly added first proviso to Section 183(6)(a) provides that statements are to be recorded by a woman Magistrate as far as practicable.A newly added second proviso to Section 183(6)(a) provides for mandatory recording of a statement of witness by Magistrate relating to the offences punishable with imprisonment for ten years or more, imprisonment for life, or with death.The fourth proviso to Section 183(6)(a) provides for the recording of the statement of a temporarily or permanently mentally or physically disabled person through audio-video electronic means.

184.subject: Medical examination of the victim of rape.
184.comparison_summary: "without delay" is replaced by "Within a period of seven days" for medical practitioner to forward the report to Investigating officer.

189.comparison_summary: The words "with or without sureties" are excluded and "or bail bond" are added.

190.comparison_summary: New proviso is added to subsection (1) regarding taking security from accused not in custody before the magistrate and mandate the Magistrate to accept the same.

194.subject: Police to enquire and report on suicide, etc.
194.comparison_summary: In subsection (2) "forthwith" is replaced by "within twenty- four hours" for sending the report to DM and SDM. The word “man” is replaced by “person”.

195.subject: Power to summon persons.
195.comparison_summary: Proviso to Subsection (1) of 195 provides immunity to male persons under the age of fifteen years or above the age of sixty years, or a woman or a mentally or physically disabled persons or a person with acute illness shall not be required to attend at place other than the place where such person resides. However, they can attend at police station willingly.

196.comparison_summary: The words "Judicial Magistrate" are replaced by "Magistrate", and Metropolitan Magistrate is excluded.

202.comparison_summary: The words "electronic communications" added. BNS sections replace corresponding IPC sections.

205.subject: Power to order cases to be tried in different sessions divisions.

206.subject: High Court to decide, in case of doubt, district where inquiry or trial shall take place.

207.comparison_summary: The words "with or without sureties" are replaced by "or bail bond".

208.subject: Offence committed outside India.
208.comparison_summary: The words "or where the offence is registered in India" are added.

209.subject: Receipt of evidence relating to offences committed outside India.
209.comparison_summary: The words "either in physical form or in electronic form" are added.

210.subject: Cognizance of offences by Magistrates.
210.comparison_summary: The words "including any complaint filed by a person authorised under any special law" and "submitted in any mode including electronic mode " are added in clause (a ) and (b ) of subsection (1).

212.subject: Making over of cases to Magistrates.

213.subject: Cognizance of offences by Courts of Session.

214.subject: Additional Sessions Judges to try cases made over to them.
214.comparison_summary: No change except words "or Assistant sessions Judge" are excluded.

215.comparison_summary: BNS sections replace corresponding IPC sections. The words "or of some other public servant who is authorised by the concerned public servant so to do" are added.

216.subject: Procedure for witnesses in case of threatening, etc.

218.subject: Prosecution of Judges and public servants.
218.comparison_summary: The BNS sections replace the corresponding IPC sections. A second proviso to 218(1)(b) is added, which provides that such Government shall take a decision within a period of one hundred and twenty days from the date of receipt of the request for sanction, and in case it fails to do so, the sanction shall be deemed to have been accorded by such Government. This will curb the tendency to help an accused p ublic servant by such delay tactics.

219.subject: Prosecution for offences against marriage.
219.comparison_summary: The Word "child" replaces "minor" and "child" also replaces "person under the age of 18 years", "is an idiot, or a lunatic" is replaced by "is of unsound mind or is having intellectual disability requiring higher support needs".

220.subject: Prosecution of offences under section 498A of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023.

222.subject: Prosecution for defamation.
222.comparison_summary: BNS Chapters and sections replace corresponding IPC Chapters and sections. "child" replaces "under the age of eighteen years " or is of unsound mind replaces "or is having intellectual disability or is an idiot or a lunatic".

223.subject: Examination of complainant.
223.comparison_summary: The newly added first proviso to Section 223(1) provides that no cognizance of an offence shall be taken by the Magistrate without giving the accused an opportunity of being heard. A new sub-section (2) of Section 223 provides that a Magistrate shall not take cognizance on a complaint against a public servant for any offence alleged to have been committed in the course of the discharge of his official functions or duties unless—(a) such public servant is given an opportunity to make assertions as to the situation that led to the incident so alleged; and (b) a report containing facts and circumstances of the incident from the officer superior to such a public servant is r eceived.

224.subject: Procedure by Magistrate not competent to take cognizance of the case.

227.subject: Issue of process.
227.comparison_summary: The new proviso to sub-section (1) of Section 227 provides that summons or warrant may also be issued through electronic means.

228.subject: Magistrate may dispense with personal attendance of accused.

229.subject: Special summons in cases of petty offence.
229.comparison_summary: "pleader" is replaced by "advocate." The threshold amount of fine for the purpose of the definition of "petty offence" is increased by this section from one thousand rupees to five thousand rupees. The monetary limit of the fine specified in summons is increased from one thousand rupees to five thousand rupees. “the Motor Vehicles Act, 1939 (59 of 1939)” is replaced by “the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 (59 of 1988).”.

230.subject: Supply to the accused of copy of police report and other documents.
230.comparison_summary: This section states that the documents are to be supplied free of cost to the accused without any delay, and in no case beyond 14 days from the date of production or appearance of the accused, and should also be provided to the victim if represented by an advocate. The last proviso states that the supply of documents in electronic form shall be considered as duly furnished.

231.subject: Supply of copies of statements and documents to accused in other cases triable by Court of Session.
231.comparison_summary: Last proviso states that supply of documents to accused in electronic form shall be considered as duly furnished. "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".

232.subject: Commitment of case to Court of Session when offence is triable exclusively by it.
232.comparison_summary: Two new provisos are added herein. By first proviso proceedings have been made time-bound. The proceedings under this section shall be completed within a period of ninety days from the date of taking cognizance, and such period may be extended, by the Magistrate for a period not exceeding one hundred eighty days for the reasons to be recorded in writing. Second proviso states that any application filed before the Magistrate by the accused or the victim or any person authorised by such person in a case triable by Court of Session, shall be forwarded to the Court of Session with the committal of the case.

245.subject: When offence proved included in offence charged.

258.subject: Judgment of acquittal or conviction.
258.comparison_summary: New addition to subsection (1) for time bound disposal- After hearing arguments and points of law (if any), the Judge shall give a judgement in the case, “as soon as possible, within a period of thirty days from the date of completion of arguments, which may be extended, to a period of fortyfive days for reasons to be recorded in writing”.

260.subject: Procedure in cases instituted under sib-section 2 of section 222.
260.comparison_summary: Upper limit of compensation amount increased from one thousand rupees to five thousand rupees in sub-section (4).

262.subject: When accused shall be discharged.

Examination of accused through audio- video electronic means can be done as added in sub-section (2).

263.subject: Framing of charge.
263.comparison_summary: Time limit for framing of charge against the accused is added within a period of sixty days from the date of first hearing on charge.

266.subject: Evidence for defence.
266.comparison_summary: New second proviso to Section 266(2) provides that the examination of witnesses may be done by audio-video electronic means at the designated place to be notified by t he State Government.

269.subject: Procedure where accused is not discharged.
269.comparison_summary: The newly added subsection (7) provides that if the attendance of the prosecution witnesses under sub-sections (5) and (6) cannot be secured for cross-examination, it shall be deemed that such witness has not been examined for not b eing available and Magistrate proceed further.

272.subject: Absence of complainant.
272.comparison_summary: It is added that the Magistrate may “after giving thirty days’ time to the complainant to be present before proceeding further.

273.subject: Compensation for accusation without reasonable cause.
273.comparison_summary: BNS Sections replace corresponding IPC sections. Compensation amount under sub-section (6) increased from "one hundred rupees" to “two thousand rupees”.

274.subject: Substance of accusation to be stated.
274.comparison_summary: The new proviso to Section 274 provides that if the Magistrate considers the accusation groundless, he shall, after recording reasons in writing, release the accused, and s uch release shall have the effect of discharge.

276.subject: Conviction on plea of guilty in absence of accused in petty cases.

279.subject: Non-appearance or death of complainant.
279.comparison_summary: The words "after giving thirty days' time to the complainant to be present" are added. "pleader" is replaced by "advocate".
